source.title: How do you feel--now? The anterior insula and human awareness

confidence_rationale: Authoritative review (6000+ citations) establishing the posterior-to-anterior insular gradient: posterior insula encodes primary interoceptive representation (direct, high-resolution, sensory-discriminative), anterior insula re-represents this as integrated subjective state (affective, homeostatic). This gradient maps directly to z_harm_s (PIC) vs z_harm_a (AIC). Craig's framework extends his earlier 2002/2003 work already in this review by adding the meta-representation and awareness dimensions, strengthening the case that z_harm_a is not merely a smoothed version of z_harm_s but a qualitatively different representation.
failure_signatures: The review focuses on interoception and awareness broadly, not specifically on nociceptive dual streams. Pain is one of many interoceptive modalities discussed.
The anterior insula framework concerns subjective awareness, not directly the motivational-urgency function that z_harm_a serves in REE.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The dorsal posterior insula (PIC) contains a primary interoceptive representation that engenders distinct, highly resolved feelings from the body including pain, temperature, itch, and visceral sensations. The right anterior insula (AIC) generates a meta-representation of this activity, providing the basis for the subjective image of the material self as a feeling (sentient) entity — emotional awareness.
mapping.ree_translation: PIC primary interoceptive representation = z_harm_s: immediate, high-resolution, sensory-discriminative encoding of harm proximity/intensity. AIC meta-representation = z_harm_a: integrated, accumulated homeostatic state that incorporates temporal context and body-state history. The posterior-to-anterior gradient is not merely spatial smoothing but a qualitative transformation — from resolved sensation to integrated affect — matching SD-011's claim that z_harm_a carries fundamentally different information than z_harm_s.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Craig's framework is about interoception and awareness broadly, not specifically about nociceptive dual-stream architecture. The AIC meta-representation concerns subjective experience and sentience, which is broader than z_harm_a's specific function as motivational urgency signal for E3 commit gating. The step from 'awareness substrate' to 'motivational drive input' requires additional mechanistic argument.
mapping.source_context: Review synthesizing human neuroimaging, lesion, and anatomical studies. Primarily concerned with anterior insular cortex as neural correlate of awareness, with pain as one of several interoceptive modalities.

confidence_components.notes: Extremely high source quality (Nat Rev Neurosci, Craig is the leading authority on insular interoception, 6000+ citations). Mapping fidelity good but not perfect: the posterior/anterior gradient is compelling for SD-011 but the AIC function in Craig's framework (awareness) is broader than z_harm_a's function (motivational urgency). Transfer risk low: directly about human pain/interoception neuroanatomy.
